This feedback doc includes bugs which I found during the onboarding process of Meshery in DOKS. I’ve tried to include a screenshot of every bug I found. Feel free to reach out if any questions.

1. I installed Meshery through mesheryctl in my k8s cluster. Once that was done, I ran `mesheryctl system dashboard` and landed on the login page. On the first login attempt I get this error, but the second attempt logs in successfully.

   ![][image1]

2. On that second attempt, after I clicked the "Back to login" button, I ended up logging into Layer5 Cloud instead of Meshery. These two are the biggest blockers for anyone trying Meshery, because as a user you can't even get into Meshery on the second attempt. There's also a case where a user needs to sign up first, I didn't test that one. I think that works fine since Narsi didn’t mentioned about these errors with me.

3.  Here it shows nice k8s connections, which seems a little weird. My assumption is that it's persisting my old connections and showing them here. But as we discussed last time, I sometimes wonder if it'd be nicer to only see connections that are active. I also have a question on tenancy. Connections have to live under an environment. If a user doesn't have access to that environment, will they still see those k8s connections in the list/dropdown? In other words, does that dropdown follow tenancy, or was it not meant to? Either way, this feels like a low-hanging item given our focus and time constraints.

4. When I try to create a connection, my dashboard is empty on the first run \- I click Create Connection, then k8s. I feel like if Meshery is pre-installed in my k8s, it should auto-detect and show me "here's one discovered cluster" by default if any otherwise you can upload a kubeconfig, which we can do right now. I'm mentioning this because we already have this feature, right? We already do that. Is it because we stopped pre-installing the operator and broker? 

   Even after I created a new connection, I still can't ping it. When I check, it says operator disabled and meshsync disabled. But as you can see in another screenshot, both the operator and meshsync have been running for 45h \- and I made my k8s connection just 1 hr ago.

   One more thing: my kubeconfig file contains a long list of clusters. I'm flagging this because I'm not sure how Meshery does cluster discovery. Is there anything else I need to do? If there's a step we need to take for security reasons, I think we should surface it in the "i" icon so people know to check the docs. If there's a step the user is supposed to do, we should make it visible; if there isn't, then this might be a bug. My worry is that if there is a required step like exposing a service, users won't dig into the docs on their own, because the UI doesn't signal "the next step is yours." They'll just wonder why it isn't working: *I did my part, what's left?* This is similar to what Narsi said.

   

5. In the notification center, I'm pretty sure I had a lot of notifications under the error category after the failed connection requests. When I first opened the notification center, it did show me those error notifications, but within 3 seconds they disappeared and I could only see unread messages. I thought it might be a filter being applied in the notification center, but no \- it started showing 0 error messages. Even when I apply the error filter, I can't find them at all.

[image1]: <data:image/png;base64,>
